items.title: Economist blames ACT's fiscal deterioration entirely on 'policy decisions'

items.title: 'How do I survive this?' The financial burden of Catalina's cancer treatment

children.content: Research from the Cancer Council shows more than 38 per cent of cancer patients lose their income during treatment. For Catalina Alcazar, it was an unexpected extra burden as she faced treatment for a tumour in her oesophagus.

items.title: Woman killed in bus crash asked to be organ donor just days earlier

children.content: Uqasha Imran was fatally injured when a bus driver struck her on her motorcycle in 2025. Now, her family are promoting the importance of organ donation while helping other young athletes like her.

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

items.title: RBA warns Australia risks slipping into '1990s recession' as oil shock mounts

children.content: The Reserve Bank is growing increasingly anxious about price pressures building in the economy and its chief economist has raised the risk of a recession in post-budget remarks.

articles.title: 'The party had to end': Young investors torn on budget tax reform

children.content: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.

articles.title: Tax breaks reward those who can afford to take risks. What about the rest of us?

children.content: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

items.title: Will the war in Iran make Australia use less plastic?

children.content: Could the war in Iran change the way we recycle back home?
Australia mostly relies on importing plastic from overseas.
As well as petrol and diesel, the Strait of Hormuz is a key delivery route for a crucial backbone of plastic production. But, that's now cut off.
It's led to a growing conversation about how Australia might transition to fully recycling plastic packaging onshore.

image.altText: TN_PlasticIran

items.title: Why Sam Walker is the great unknown of State of Origin I

children.content: Sam Walker has always seemed like a player destined for the highest levels of rugby league, but ahead of his State of Origin debut, it's impossible to know what comes next for the new Queensland halfback.

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.

items.title: Refunds but no price caps for ripped-off older Australians

children.content: Support at Home program price caps, due to start in July, have been deferred indefinitely. Hundreds of thousands of older people rely on the federal government program, which provides help with cleaning, showering and clinical supports like nursing.
